金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)
[[Category:Kk]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] [[ja:v]]

金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)
[[Category:Kk]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] [[ja:v]] {{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | Image = {{画像募集中|cat=恵那市}} | Location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | Coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | AlternateName = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== Annual Festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Kimura Teru|title=Gifu Prefecture's Major Shrine Pilgrimage Guide|publisher=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 Kotohira Shrine (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]

金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)
[[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] [[ja:v]] {{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | Image = {{画像募集中|cat=恵那市}} | Location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | Coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | AlternateName = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== Annual Festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Kimura Teru|title=Gifu Prefecture's Major Shrine Pilgrimage Guide|publisher=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 Kotohira Shrine (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]

金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)
[[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] [[ja:v]] {{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | image = {{画像募集中|cat=恵那市}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | Coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | AlternateName = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== Annual Festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Kimura Teru|title=Gifu Prefecture's Major Shrine Pilgrimage Guide|publisher=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 Kotohira Shrine (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]

The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== Annual Festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

[[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | AlternateName = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== Annual Festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Kimura Teru|title=Gifu Prefecture's Major Shrine Pilgrimage Guide|publisher=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}}

[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] [[ja:v]] {{Shrine | Name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | image = {{画像募集中|cat=恵那市}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | Coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | AlternateName = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] [[ja:v]] {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{画像募集中|cat=恵那市}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Kimura Teru|title=Gifu Prefecture's Major Shrine Pilgrimage Guide|publisher=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 Kotohira Shrine (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]

[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] [[ja:v]] {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{画像募集中|cat=恵那市}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Kimura Teru|title=Gifu Prefecture's Major Shrine Pilgrimage Guide|publisher=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 Kotohira Shrine (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

Shrine (Ena Yamaoka)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] [[ja:v]] {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{画像募集中|cat=恵那市}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Kimura Teru|title=Gifu Prefecture's Major Shrine Pilgrimage Guide|publisher=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 Kotohira Shrine (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]

(Ena Yamaoka)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] [[ja:v]] {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{画像募集中|cat=恵那市}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

(Ena Yamaoka)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] [[ja:v]] {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{画像募集中|cat=恵那市}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

(Ena Yamaoka)]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] [[ja:v]] {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{画像募集中|cat=恵那市}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{画像募集中|cat=恵那市}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Kimura Teru|title=Gifu Prefecture's Major Shrine Pilgrimage Guide|publisher=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 Kotohira Shrine (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=恵那市}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Kimura Teru|title=Gifu Prefecture's Major Shrine Pilgrimage Guide|publisher=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 Kotohira Shrine (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]

[[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Ena Yamaoka)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Ena Yamaoka)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] [[ja:v]] {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=恵那市}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

[[Category:qq-Konpira Shrine (Ena Yamaoka)-qq]] [[Category:Konpira Shrine (Ena Yamaoka)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] [[ja:v]] {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=恵那市}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

Yamaoka)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] [[ja:v]] {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=恵那市}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

Yamaoka)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] [[ja:v]] {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=恵那市}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

Yamaoka)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] [[ja:v]] {{Shrine | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=恵那市}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

{{image募集中|cat=恵那市}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

Yamaoka)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] [[ja:v]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=恵那市}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

Yamaoka)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] [[ja:v]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=恵那市}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

Yamaoka)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] [[ja:v]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=恵那市}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

Yamaoka)]][[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] [[ja:v]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = {{image募集中|cat=恵那市}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Kimura Teru|title=Gifu Prefecture's Major Shrine Pilgrimage Guide|publisher=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 Kotohira Shrine (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]

Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|ja|延宝}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena City|ja|恵那市}}, [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|ja|讃岐国}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大物主}} and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara Village (Gifu)|ja|原村}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira Shrine (Kagawa)|ja|金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|ja|宝暦}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.
