<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q124496758]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q124496758]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} 金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原).jpg | location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[:en:Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Ena|WD=Q819653}} }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within [[:en:Ena, Gifu|Ena City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] of [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōkuninushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) and [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]].<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of [[:en:Hara, Nagano|Hara Village (Gifu)]], who brought a division of the deity from [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]]. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|1=Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675962}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ena City Community Bus|1=Ena City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492449}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Kimura Teru|title=Gifu Prefecture's Major Shrine Pilgrimage Guide|publisher=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 Kotohira Shrine (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q124496758]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q124496758]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} 金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原).jpg | location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[:en:Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Ena|WD=Q819653}} }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within [[:en:Ena, Gifu|Ena City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] of [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōkuninushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) and [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]].<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of [[:en:Hara, Nagano|Hara Village (Gifu)]], who brought a division of the deity from [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]]. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|1=Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675962}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ena City Community Bus|1=Ena City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492449}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

== Bibliography == * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Kimura Teru|title=Gifu Prefecture's Major Shrine Pilgrimage Guide|publisher=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}}

The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q124496758]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q124496758]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} 金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原).jpg | location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[:en:Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Ena|WD=Q819653}} }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within [[:en:Ena, Gifu|Ena City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] of [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōkuninushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) and [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]].<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of [[:en:Hara, Nagano|Hara Village (Gifu)]], who brought a division of the deity from [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]]. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|1=Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675962}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ena City Community Bus|1=Ena City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492449}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Kimura Teru|title=Gifu Prefecture's Major Shrine Pilgrimage Guide|publisher=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 Kotohira Shrine (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]

In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|1=Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675962}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ena City Community Bus|1=Ena City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492449}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Kimura Teru|title=Gifu Prefecture's Major Shrine Pilgrimage Guide|publisher=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}}

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within [[:en:Ena, Gifu|Ena City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] of [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōkuninushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) and [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]].<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of [[:en:Hara, Nagano|Hara Village (Gifu)]], who brought a division of the deity from [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]]. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|1=Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675962}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ena City Community Bus|1=Ena City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492449}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原).jpg | location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[:en:Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]] | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ([[:en:Enpō|Enpō]] 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Ena|WD=Q819653}} }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within [[:en:Ena, Gifu|Ena City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is a branch shrine of the [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] of [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōkuninushi]] ([[Konpira Gongen]]) and [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]].<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of [[:en:Hara, Nagano|Hara Village (Gifu)]], who brought a division of the deity from [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]]. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the [[:en:Hōreki|Hōreki]] era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|1=Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675962}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]]
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ena City Community Bus|1=Ena City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492449}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Kimura Teru|title=Gifu Prefecture's Major Shrine Pilgrimage Guide|publisher=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 Kotohira Shrine (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q124496758]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q124496758]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} 金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原).jpg | location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu}}, [[:en:Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku}} | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Ena|WD=Q819653}} }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena, Gifu|en|Ena, Gifu|WD=Q819653|lt=Ena City}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira-gū}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōkuninushi}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara, Nagano|en|Hara, Nagano|WD=Q1203041|lt=Hara Village (Gifu)}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira-gū}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|en|First Sino-Japanese War|WD=Q178687|lt=First Sino-Japanese War}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|1=Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675962}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku}}
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ena City Community Bus|1=Ena City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492449}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Kimura Teru|title=Gifu Prefecture's Major Shrine Pilgrimage Guide|publisher=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}}
== External Links == * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 Kotohira Shrine (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]

In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|en|First Sino-Japanese War|WD=Q178687|lt=First Sino-Japanese War}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|1=Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675962}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku}}
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ena City Community Bus|1=Ena City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492449}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.

'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena, Gifu|en|Ena, Gifu|WD=Q819653|lt=Ena City}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. == Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara, Nagano|en|Hara, Nagano|WD=Q1203041|lt=Hara Village (Gifu)}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira-gū}}. == Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]]
金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原).jpg | location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, [[:en:Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}} | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|WD=Q1145735|lt=Enpō|1=Enpō}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Ena|WD=Q819653|1=Ena, Gifu}} }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena, Gifu|en|Ena, Gifu|WD=Q819653|lt=Ena City|1=Ena, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira-gū|1=Kotohira-gū}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōmononushi}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara, Nagano|en|Hara, Nagano|WD=Q1203041|lt=Hara Village (Gifu)|1=Hara, Nagano}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira-gū|1=Kotohira-gū}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|WD=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki|1=Hōreki}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|en|First Sino-Japanese War|WD=Q178687|lt=First Sino-Japanese War|1=First Sino-Japanese War}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War|1=Russo-Japanese War}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|1=Q11675962|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675962}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}}
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ena City Community Bus|1=Ena City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492449}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|language=Japanese|editor=Kimura Teru|title=Gifu Prefecture's Major Shrine Pilgrimage Guide|publisher=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)]]
金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原)
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Kotohira Shrine (金刀比羅神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:金刀比羅神社 (恵那市山岡町原).jpg | location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, [[:en:Ena City|Ena]], [[Yamaoka Town|Yamaoka]], Hara 682 | coordinates = | Deities = [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]], {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|qid=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}} | Classification = Former [[Village Shrine]]; [[Gold Ribbon Shrine]] | Established = [[1673]] ({{ill|Enpō|en|Enpō|qid=Q1145735|lt=Enpō|1=Enpō}} 1) | MainHall = | Alternatename = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = 2nd Sunday of November | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Ena|qid=Q819653|1=Ena, Gifu}} }}
'''Kotohira Shrine''' (金刀比羅神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} shrine located in [[Yamaoka Town]] within {{ill|Ena, Gifu|en|Ena, Gifu|qid=Q819653|lt=Ena City|1=Ena, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a branch shrine of the {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|qid=Q94760|lt=Kotohira-gū|1=Kotohira-gū}} of {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|qid=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}}, established in 1673 (Enpō 1) by local villagers. The shrine honors {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōkuninushi|1=Ōmononushi}} ([[Konpira Gongen]]) and {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|qid=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}}.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁">[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1630&shrname=金刀比羅神社 金刀比羅神社 (Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau)]</ref>
== Overview == * The shrine was originally built by the villagers of {{ill|Hara, Nagano|en|Hara, Nagano|qid=Q1203041|lt=Hara Village (Gifu)|1=Hara, Nagano}}, who brought a division of the deity from {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|qid=Q94760|lt=Kotohira-gū|1=Kotohira-gū}}. In 1764 (Meiwa 1), a new main hall was constructed.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * During the {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|qid=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki|1=Hōreki}} era, a villager named Kogiso Kihē made 33 pilgrimages to the main shrine in Sanuki, bringing back sacred earth to enshrine at the local shrine.<ref name="岐阜県神社庁" /> * The grounds feature memorials for soldiers from the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|en|First Sino-Japanese War|qid=Q178687|lt=First Sino-Japanese War|1=First Sino-Japanese War}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|qid=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War|1=Russo-Japanese War}}, and other conflicts, commemorating the war dead from the former {{ill|Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|ja|鶴岡村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugaoka Village (Gifu)|1=Q11675962|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11675962}} in Ena District. * In 1963 (Shōwa 38), the shrine was designated a Gold Ribbon Shrine by the Gifu Prefecture Shrine Bureau.<ref>{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=153}}</ref>
== Deities == * [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Kami]] * {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|qid=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}}
== festivals == * Spring Festival: 2nd Sunday of April * Autumn Festival: 2nd Sunday of November
== Access == * By Bus: Take the {{ill|Ena City Community Bus|ja|恵那市自主運行バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ena City Community Bus|1=Ena City Community Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11492449}}, Yamaoka District [52] Fukigoe Line, and get off at "Kotohira Shrine" bus stop. The shrine is a 2-minute walk.
